exemplar.title: How does Edgar Allan Poe use literary resources to create a horrifying atmosphere in "The Tell-Tale Heart" and "The masque of the Red Death"?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Edgar Allan Poe use literary resources to create a horrifying atmosphere in "The Tell-Tale Heart" and "The masque of the Red Death"?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused on two texts and three literary techniques, and is consistently revisited in chapter headings and the conclusion.
The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with a clear and appropriate purpose maintained throughout the investigation.
Chapters are organized around the stated focal elements (symbolism, point of view, descriptive language), demonstrating coherence between purpose and structure.
weaknesses: Methodology remains implicit rather than explicitly framed; a dedicated description of sourceâselection criteria and analytical procedures is still needed.
Occasional lapses in linking theoretical discussion to textual analysis suggest minor scope imperfections that could be smoothed with more explicit transitions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors of the top band: the topic and research question are exceptionally well communicated and remain central throughout. Although the methodological rationale is not fully explicit, the studentâs informed selection of sources and sustained organizational coherence justify awarding the maximum 6 points for Focus and Method.

strengths: Coherent and clear subject knowledge of Poeâs gothic techniques, distinguishing key concepts such as horror vs. terror.
Primary quotations are generally wellâchosen and integrated into the analytical discussion.
Literary terminology (symbolism, firstâperson narrator, descriptive language) is defined and applied accurately across the essay.
weaknesses: Overâreliance on generalist sources (e.g., repeated Britannica entries) limits the depth of secondary engagement.
Some definitions (e.g., of âhorrorâ) lack precise scholarly citation, and descriptive language analysis would benefit from more specific textual examples.
Terminology occasionally defaults to the vague phrase âliterary resources,â detracting from precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear subject knowledge and mostly appropriate application of sources, placing the work solidly in the 3â4 band. Weaknesses in source diversity and critical deployment preclude a topâband mark, so 4 of 6 points accurately reflect the Quality of Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Analysis consistently links primary evidence (e.g., the âvulture eye,â ebony clock) to interpretative claims about atmosphere creation.
Comparative evaluation across both stories shows coherent argumentation and some depth in assessing horror techniques.
The conclusion acknowledges limitations and restates how findings address the research question, demonstrating evaluative intent.
weaknesses: Critical engagement with secondary sources is limited; evaluation of source authority and counterâarguments is largely absent.
Some analytical passages remain surfaceâlevel, reiterating accepted positions without probing alternative readings.
The final synthesis is truncated, indicating incomplete development of evaluative discussion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the studentâs analysis moves beyond description and offers reasoned argumentation, the lack of deeper source evaluation and limited engagement with alternative perspectives places the work in the lower half of the 7â9 band. Awarding 7 of 12 captures this âgoodâ but improvable level of critical thinking.

strengths: The report includes essential layout elements (title page, table of contents, inâtext citations, and page numbering).
Headings and subâheadings are generally clear, supporting reader navigation through the argument.
weaknesses: The investigation exceeds the 4 000âword limit and lacks an abstract, reducing compliance with IB structural expectations.
Inconsistent citation style and typographic errors (e.g., truncated headings, spacing issues) detract from overall polish.
Missing frontâmatter elements and occasional abrupt transitions signal incomplete adherence to presentation requirements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets basic structural and layout conventions but falls short of complete EE presentation standards (abstract, wordâlimit compliance, consistent formatting). A band of 1â2 is appropriate, and 2 points accurately reflect this level of presentation quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââHow does Edgar Allan Poe use literary resources to create a horrifying atmosphere in âThe Tell-Tale Heartâ and âThe Masque of the Red Deathâ?ââis prominently and unambiguously stated.  It is sufficiently narrow (two texts, three named techniques) and is revisited in every chapter heading as well as in the conclusion, demonstrating a sustained link between question and discussion.  Minor breadth remains in the phrase âliterary resources,â but overall clarity, focus and integration with the essayâs structure justify placement in the upper band.

Analytical sections move beyond simple description by selecting textual evidence and linking it to the creation of atmosphere; for instance, the discussion of the âvulture eyeâ and the ebony clock makes logical interpretative claims.  Yet much of the commentary remains surface-level, reiterating plot or accepted critical positions without probing alternative readings or wider literary implications.  Occasional repetition and limited synthesis constrain the depth of insight, corresponding to a solid but not exceptional performance in the 4-6 band.

Primary quotations from the stories are woven into the analysis and are usually pertinent to the point being made.  Secondary material is referenced, but it is often explanatory rather than critically engaged, and sources such as encyclopaedia articles dominate.  Consequently, while application is mostly relevant, it lacks the consistent appropriateness and critical deployment expected in the highest bands, aligning the strand with the middle band (3-4) at a score of 3.

The work contains a title page, table of contents and in-text citations, and page numbers are indicated, demonstrating attention to basic layout conventions.  Nonetheless, the piece exceeds the 4 000-word limit and some formatting (e.g., inconsistent citation style, occasional spacing and typographic issues) detract from overall presentation quality.  Given that several elements are correct but key requirements are not fully met, the submission sits within the 1-2 band, meriting a score of 2.

The essay conveys a generally sound understanding of Poeâs gothic techniques, distinguishing between horror and terror and explaining symbolism, narrative perspective and descriptive language with reference to the texts.  Explanations are coherent, though occasionally simplistic or derivative, and some biographical details verge on summary.  Overall, the student demonstrates clear but not fully sophisticated subject knowledge, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The essay follows a conventional structure with introduction, two analytical chapters, conclusion and references, and a table of contents.  However, required EE elements such as an abstract are missing, and headings sometimes repeat information.  Transitions between sections are occasionally abrupt, and the over-extended word count suggests imperfect structural discipline.  These features align with a âgenerally appropriateâ but incomplete structure, placing it in the 1-2 band at the top score of 2.

The research base is adequate: a range of secondary sources is cited, and the primary texts are analysed in detail.  Nevertheless, many sources are generalist websites, and there is limited evidence of critical scholarship or evaluation of source quality.  The material employed is generally relevant to the question but lacks depth and rigour, situating it in the 4-6 band at the lower score of 4.

The student states the general topic (the creation of horrifying atmospheres in literature) and then immediately narrows it to an examination of Poeâs two short stories.  The purposeâto explore the role of symbolism, point of view and descriptive language in generating horrorâis introduced explicitly in both the title and the opening paragraphs, and these same focal elements organise the subsequent chapters.  Although the phrasing of âliterary resourcesâ is slightly awkward and the  word-limit overrun hints at imperfect scoping, the reader is never in doubt about what is being investigated or why.  This clear, appropriate and consistently maintained focus matches the descriptors of the 5-6 band.

The conclusion restates findings and briefly acknowledges limitations, showing some attempt at evaluation.  Within chapters, the student offers reasoned, if predictable, links between techniques and effect.  However, counter-arguments, critical perspectives and self-questioning are largely absent, and the evaluation of sources or methodology is minimal.  The discussion therefore meets the descriptors for a âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ level, warranting a score of 4 within the 4-6 band.

The methodology is described implicitly rather than through a formal section: primary texts are identified, secondary sources are said to âsupport the discussion,â and the essay is organised around three analytical lenses.  A reasonable range of sources (scholarly articles, encyclopaedia entries, web essays) is listed, showing some informed selection, yet there is limited explanation of how or why particular sources were chosen, and the bibliography reveals an over-reliance on general web materials.  These features place the work comfortably in the 3-4 band, at the top of that band for its generally coherent but not fully explicit methodological planning.

Key literary termsâsymbolism, first-person narrator, descriptive languageâare defined and used accurately throughout, and the student differentiates between âhorrorâ and âterror.â  However, critical vocabulary is sometimes repetitive and occasionally imprecise (e.g., frequent use of âliterary resourcesâ without clarification).  The terminology is mostly correct but not consistently sophisticated, fitting the mid-band descriptor.

annotations.comment: Contextual information is largely a biographical summary; integrate critical scholarship to analyze how Poeâs life influenced specific literary techniques.
The subsection on Poeâs literary style is clearly introduced, focusing the reader on relevant analytical categories.
The research question is prominently and unambiguously stated, specifying two texts and three techniques, which establishes a clear focus and organizational framework for the entire investigation.
The declared word count of 4067 exceeds the 4000-word limit; condense or eliminate around 67 words to comply with requirements.
Contextual description of the castle setting lacks a citation for the primary text; include precise parenthetical references to support credibility.
The symbolic interpretation of color progression is strong but would benefit from direct textual evidence regarding guestsâ awareness levels.
Insightful discussion of the ebony clockâs symbolism demonstrates an understanding of how time motifs intensify horror.
Several headings appear truncated or contain typographical errors (e.g., âstorieâ); proofread and ensure consistent formatting of entries.
The abstract is absent from the TOC; add an entry for the abstract section to reflect the complete structure.
The introduction restates the research question clearly and outlines the analytical lenses, establishing a focused approach to the investigation.
Methodology is described implicitly; include a dedicated section explaining source selection criteria and analytical procedures to strengthen methodological transparency.
The bibliography repeats the same Britannica source for multiple footnotes; diversify secondary sources and ensure accurate referencing to support claims effectively.
Including Poeâs personal tragedies offers valuable context for thematic analysis of death and loss in his stories.
Overreliance on a single biography.com source suggests limited evaluation of source quality; include peer-reviewed criticism to strengthen research quality.
The explanation of symbolismâs function in horror literature is clear and demonstrates appropriate use of terminology.
The citation for character perspective lacks author credentials and date, reducing source credibility; refine citation format and evaluate source authority.
The chapter heading is clearly labeled, supporting a coherent structure and guiding the reader through the progression of your argument.
An abstract is missing from the front matter; include a concise abstract summarizing the research scope, methodology, and key findings to meet IB structure expectations.
The table of contents is comprehensive, listing chapters and sub-sections with clear page references, facilitating reader navigation.
The rationale connecting Poeâs legacy and human psychological responses to horror is well explained, demonstrating clear subject knowledge and relevance.
The definition of âhorrorâ lacks a supporting citation and precise scholarly framing; provide a referenced definition to clarify conceptual distinctions.
The subsection on the role of literary resources is well signposted, aiding reader orientation and structural clarity.
The transition from theoretical discussion to analysis of Poeâs examples is abrupt; add linking sentences to show how concepts will be applied to the texts.
The section on descriptive language is generic; integrate specific textual examples to illustrate how Poeâs diction produces horror effects.
Reference to Poeâs âPhilosophy of Compositionâ lacks engagement with its argument; critically analyze its relevance to atmosphere creation rather than simple summary.
Engaging with multiple social-science journal articles shows breadth in research, supporting a nuanced understanding of symbolic elements.
Excellent integration of primary quotations with interpretative commentary; this deepens the psychological analysis of the narratorâs obsession.
Repeated quotations of the same eye imagery could be consolidated for conciseness, freeing space for deeper critical insight.
The final sentence of this paragraph is incomplete; ensure each analytic point concludes with a cohesive explanation of its effect.
The concluding paragraph on point of view would benefit from explicitly revisiting the research question wording to reinforce focus.
The conclusion starts but is truncated; ensure full synthesis of findings and direct answers to the research question before closing.
The transition into the first story analysis is abrupt; add a brief bridging sentence to connect general symbolism discussion to âThe Tell-Tale Heart.â
The first synthesis paragraph lacks an explicit link to the original research question phrasing; reiterate terminology to reinforce focus.
Comparative analysis of symbolism across both stories demonstrates evaluative depth and a coherent argument about horror techniques.
Lengthy paragraph discussing point of view lacks parenthetical page references; add citations for all primary quotations to meet formatting conventions.
Proposing analysis of additional Poe stories as future work shows initiative in extending the research beyond current scope.
The overall concluding reflection effectively summarizes how Poeâs techniques function across psychological and visual dimensions.
The concluding sentence is cut off mid-idea; revise to present a complete evaluation of symbolism before transitioning to the next section.
The analysis of repetitive internal monologue effectively shows how the narratorâs instability creates a claustrophobic and maddening atmosphere.
The discussion of multi-sensory descriptive language vividly conveys how diction immerses readers in a fear-filled environment.
Excellent clock imagery analysis; to enhance cohesion, link this analysis back to the overarching thesis on atmosphere creation.
Repetitive phrasing at the end of the descriptive section dilutes impact; vary sentence structures to maintain reader engagement.
The âConclusionâ header deviates from previous chapter formatting (no underline); standardize heading styles throughout the document.
Acknowledging the word-count limitation demonstrates a reflective awareness of practical constraints in research writing.
Reference entries show inconsistent title capitalization and missing italics for works; standardize to a recognized citation style (e.g., MLA, APA).
Several URLs lack retrieval dates and author metadata; include full bibliographic details to enhance reliability and traceability.
